heading: Prohibition on assistance to countries that provide military equipment to terrorist states
label: 2378 Prohibition on assistance to countries that provide military equipment to terrorist states

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The President shall withhold assistance under this chapter to the government of any country that provides lethal military equipment to a country the government of which the Secretary of State has determined is a terrorist government for the purposes of section 4605(j) of title 50, or 2371 of this title.1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Applicability
children.label: 2 Applicability
children.text: The prohibition under this section with respect to a foreign government shall terminate 1 year after that government ceases to provide lethal military equipment. This section applies with respect to lethal military equipment provided under a contract entered into after .April 24, 1996
a detailed explanation of the assistance to be provided;
the estimated dollar amount of the assistance; and
an explanation of how the assistance furthers United States national interests.
